poster_title: MLP Memory: A Retriever-Pretrained Memory for Large Language Models
authors: Rubin Wei, Jiaqi Cao, Jiarui Wang, Jushi Kai, Qipeng Guo, Bowen Zhou, Zhouhan Lin

content: Decoder-only models excel in various tasks but struggle with accurate knowledge utilization.
Non-parametric RAG methods have high inference latency and shallow integration.
Parametric methods risk catastrophic forgetting and degrade general performance.
RAG methods introduce significant inference latency.
Parametric adaptation methods degrade general capabilities.
Existing memory tokens lack long-term knowledge retention.
Propose MLP Memory to internalize retrieval patterns without document access.
Pretrain MLP to imitate 𝑘NN retriever’s behavior on pretraining dataset.
Integrate pretrained MLP Memory with Transformer decoders via probability interpolation.
panels.figure_caption: Impact of KL loss weight 𝛼 on retriever imitation.

content: Evaluate on WikiText-103 and Web datasets for scaling gains.
Test on five QA benchmarks and nine general NLP tasks.
Measure inference efficiency compared to RAG and 𝑘NN-LM methods.
17.5% and 24.1% scaling gains on WikiText-103 and Web datasets.
12.3% relative improvement on QA benchmarks and 5.2 points absolute gain on NLP tasks.
Reduce hallucinations by up to 10 points on HaluEval.
panels.figure_caption: Performance and efficiency comparison on QA benchmarks.

content: MLP Memory significantly improves knowledge-intensive task performance.
Maintains constant inference speed regardless of corpus size.
Outperforms RAG and 𝑘NN-LM in both accuracy and efficiency.
MLP Memory bridges efficient inference and effective knowledge access.
Promising alternative for enhancing large language models.
Performance and efficiency gains may vary with different datasets and architectures.

Fig1.caption: Performance and efficiency comparison on QA benchmarks.
Fig1.description: 比较了在三个 QA 基准上的准确性和效率。
